# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.
# Category: datastore_security

# Elasticsearch Snapshot Configuration

# Step 1: Register a snapshot repository (S3 example)
PUT /_snapshot/my_s3_repository
{
  "type": "s3",
  "settings": {
    "bucket": "my-elasticsearch-backups",
    "region": "us-west-2",
    "role_arn": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/es-snapshot-role"
  }
}

# Step 2: Register a snapshot repository (File Share example)
PUT /_snapshot/my_file_repository
{
  "type": "fs",
  "settings": {
    "location": "/path/to/backup/directory"
  }
}

# Step 3: Create an automated snapshot policy using ILM (Index Lifecycle Management)
PUT _ilm/policy/snapshot_policy
{
  "policy": {
    "phases": {
      "hot": {
        "actions": {
          "rollover": {
            "max_size": "50gb",
            "max_age": "30d"
          }
        }
      },
      "delete": {
        "min_age": "90d",
        "actions": {
          "delete": {}
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

# Step 4: Apply the ILM policy to an index template or a specific index
PUT _template/my_template
{
  "index_patterns": ["my-index-*"],
  "settings": {
    "number_of_shards": 1,
    "number_of_replicas": 0,
    "index.lifecycle.name": "snapshot_policy"
  }
}

# Step 5: Create a snapshot manually
PUT /_snapshot/my_s3_repository/snapshot_1?wait_for_completion=true
{
  "indices": "my-index-*",
  "ignore_unavailable": true,
  "include_global_state": false
}

# Step 6: Restore a snapshot
POST /_snapshot/my_s3_repository/snapshot_1/_restore
{
  "indices": "my-index-*",
  "rename_pattern": "(.*)",
  "rename_replacement": "restored-$1"
}
